title: woodsia plummerae
intro: Woodsia plummerae, also known as Plummer's Woodsia, is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and skin infections. The health benefits of Woodsia plummerae include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and improve overall skin health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing the powdered plant with a carrier oil.
